steps.title: Convert the table to points on f
steps.content:
Do not regress the $g(x)$ values directly, because the question asks about $f(x)$. Since $g(x)=\frac{f(x)}{x+3}$, multiply each table value by $x+3$:
  • $f(-15)=3(-12)=-36$
  • $f(-6)=0(-3)=0$
  • $f(9)=5(12)=60$
So the graph of $y=f(x)$ passes through $(-15,-36)$, $(-6,0)$, and $(9,60)$.

steps.title: Fit the line in Desmos
steps.content: Enter the converted $f(x)$ points as lists, then run the linear regression $y_1 \sim m x_1+b$. Desmos gives $m=4$ and $b=24$, so $f(x)=4x+24$.

steps.title: Answer the intercept question
steps.content: The $y$-intercept of $y=f(x)$ is where $x=0$, so the point is $(0,24)$. That matches choice D.
